title: Azure Resource Manager deployment modes and idempotency
description: Explains incremental vs complete deployment modes, ARM's declarative idempotency, and how resource providers and dependsOn ordering work.
body: Use when reasoning about how ARM/Bicep applies changes. Do not assume a deployment only adds resources. Actions: know incremental mode leaves unlisted resources untouched while complete mode deletes resources not in the template; ARM is declarative and idempotent so re-applying converges to the template state; resource providers must be registered and dependsOn (or implicit references) order creation. Inspect the deployment mode and what-if before applying. Validation signals include a re-run reporting NoChange and dependent resources created in the right order. Risk boundary: complete mode against a populated resource group can delete live resources and is human-gated.
